runner: hive (alfred); 3 XIAO ESP32-S3 capturing, hearing all 9-board mesh heartbeats

args.rule: stationary node RSSI-σ ≤ 8 dBm → Infrastructure (hardened from the naive 5 dBm; hysteresis demote at 12 dBm sustained ≥2min)

expectations.observed: 49/49 settled (n>=20) neighbour-link readings σ ≤ 3.94 dBm — σ min=0.19, max=3.94, mean=0.94 dBm; 0 readings ≥8 dBm (0.0%). All settled stationary links classify Infrastructure with ~4 dBm headroom under the §2.4.1 threshold.

notes: THRESHOLD CORRECTION (specs): canon is §2.4.1's HARDENED σ≤8 dBm, not the naive 5 dBm — §2.4.1 already incorporates FINDING B (hysteresis + per-transport + stationary-noise margin). So the worst stationary link (cross-room DFR 2cab5f69, σ=3.94 dBm) clears the REAL threshold with ~4 dBm headroom = COMFORTABLE (the result is stronger than the initial 5dBm-relative report). The §2.4.1 classifier HOLDS on real ESP-NOW for stationary nodes. UNVALIDATED-ON-METAL (queued follow-up, needs a MOBILE node or RSSI-injection sweep across the 8/12 dBm band — the static co-located bench never crosses 8 dBm): the HYSTERESIS DEMOTE-band (infra→mobile only above 12 dBm sustained ≥2min) + boundary flap-prevention. The classifier-holds (promote) result stands; the demote-band is exercised when a mobility rig is available. SETTLE NOTE: low-sample neighbours (n<20) default Mobile (firmware settle-floor) = expected conservative behavior. RSSI-UNIT: esp-radio rx_control.rssi reads positive (~191-246); σ is offset-invariant so the dBm verdict holds. firmware threshold updated 5.0→8.0 to match §2.4.1.
